title: Trump softens AI order as states write the real rules - TCR 06/03/26
description:
Trump signed a narrowed AI safety order asking frontier labs to volunteer their top models for up to 30 days of government testing instead of the leaked draft's 90-day mandate, Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing to roughly 150 organizations across 15-plus countries and handed EU agency ENISA its first access to the dual-use Mythos model, and Alphabet shares fell about 4% after disclosing an $80 billion equity raise, the largest secondary offering on record.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-3-2026/

0:00 Anthropic expands Glasswing and grants ENISA Mythos access
4:50 The open-weight pack closes the frontier gap
8:40 The data center cost-and-water reckoning
11:46 Markets price the AI capital race's downside
17:28 Close

This episode covers the narrowed Trump AI executive order and the rise of binding state rules like Illinois SB 315, Anthropic's Glasswing and ENISA Mythos access, Google's data center water pledge alongside Erin Brockovich's community map and Ohio's suspended tax break, Alphabet's $80B raise and the AI capital race's first skepticism, and Nvidia's open-weight Nemotron 3 Ultra topping the open US leaderboard.

The spine of the edition is governance - the rules for the AI era being written, contested, and softened at the same speed the capability underneath compounds. A voluntary federal review arrived as a request to the same labs it would slow, while mandatory audit and hiring rules kept hardening in Illinois and New York. Anthropic became the distributor of frontier defensive cyber capability to power grids and hospitals across fifteen countries, with the order of access telling its own story. A UK regulator converted Google's all-or-nothing content scrape into an enforceable publisher opt-out. Communities ran the water, tax, and grid math the permitting process never required, and the AI capital race drew its first real skepticism as Alphabet's record raise weighed on its stock. Underneath all of it, Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Ultra landed as a free download that runs several times faster than its open peers, decentralizing capability that was supposed to concentrate.
